title: Rack Server PSU N+1 Redundancy (Cisco UCS C-Series)
spl:
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:environmental" metric_type="psu" earliest=-1h
| search rack_unit_id=*
| where oper_state!="ok" OR redundancy_state!="redundant" OR input_voltage_state!="good"
| stats latest(oper_state) as psu_state, latest(redundancy_state) as red by rack_unit_id, psu_slot
| sort rack_unit_id, psu_slot
description: A single failed PSU on a rack server without redundant feed leaves no margin before power loss. Tracking redundancy state across C-Series nodes preserves uptime for standalone HCI and database workloads.
value: A single failed PSU on a rack server without redundant feed leaves no margin before power loss. Tracking redundancy state across C-Series nodes preserves uptime for standalone HCI and database workloads.
grandmaExplanation: A single failed PSU on a rack server without redundant feed leaves no margin before power loss ŌĆö so hardware faults and capacity problems do not take services down.
implementation: (1) Map PSU inventory fields from UCSM API poll; (2) page when redundancy drops from redundant to non-redundant; (3) correlate with facility PDU events in Splunk for root cause.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
ŌĆó Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-ucs`.
ŌĆó Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=cisco_ucs` `sourcetype="cisco:ucs:environmental"` with fields `rack_unit_id`, `psu_slot`, `oper_state`, `redundancy_state`.
ŌĆó For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
(1) Map PSU inventory fields from UCSM API poll; (2) page when redundancy drops from redundant to non-redundant; (3) correlate with facility PDU events in Splunk for root cause.

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:environmental" metric_type="psu" earliest=-1h
| search rack_unit_id=*
| where oper_state!="ok" OR redundancy_state!="redundant" OR input_voltage_state!="good"
| stats latest(oper_state) as psu_state, latest(redundancy_state) as red by rack_unit_id, psu_slot
| sort rack_unit_id, psu_slot
```

Understanding this SPL

**Rack Server PSU N+1 Redundancy (Cisco UCS C-Series)** ŌĆö A single failed PSU on a rack server without redundant feed leaves no margin before power loss. Tracking redundancy state across C-Series nodes preserves uptime for standalone HCI and database workloads.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=cisco_ucs` `sourcetype="cisco:ucs:environmental"` with fields `rack_unit_id`, `psu_slot`, `oper_state`, `redundancy_state`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_cisco-ucs`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedŌĆörename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_ucs; **sourcetype**: cisco:ucs:environmental. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

ŌĆó Scopes the data: index=cisco_ucs, sourcetype="cisco:ucs:environmental", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
ŌĆó Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
ŌĆó Filters the current rows with `where oper_state!="ok" OR redundancy_state!="redundant" OR input_voltage_state!="good"` ŌĆö typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
ŌĆó `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by rack_unit_id, psu_slot** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
ŌĆó Orders rows with `sort` ŌĆö combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status grid (rack unit ├Ś PSU slot), Table (non-redundant servers), Single value (servers at risk).
visualization: Status grid (rack unit ├Ś PSU slot), Table (non-redundant servers), Single value (servers at risk).
